settings.name: Jaynesh Bhandari
settings.tagline: Full-stack developer. Three-time hackathon winner. Quiet builder of useful things.
settings.summary: Master's student in CS at Northeastern with a back-end & full-stack core. I like the moment a hard problem gets boring — when the abstraction finally clicks and the system just works. Java, Python, Flutter, SQL, and whatever the next idea calls for.
awards: Selected — TEDx Medi-Caps organizing team (7 of cohort)

quiz.q: What's Jaynesh's go-to language for a quick prototype?
quiz.funFact: Python for speed, Java for scale. COBOL… no.

quiz.q: How many hackathons has Jaynesh won?
quiz.funFact: Three. Two at Start Summit, one at Roux Institute. The trick? Sleep is overrated.

quiz.q: What did Jaynesh replace at a local business?
quiz.funFact: A Flutter + Firebase app replaced a spreadsheet that had been 'temporarily' in use for 2 years.

quiz.q: What role did Jaynesh play at TEDx?
quiz.funFact: One of 7 freshers selected. Ran the backstage on event day — improvising under pressure is a transferable skill.

quiz.q: What does Jaynesh like about hard problems?
quiz.funFact: "I like the moment a hard problem gets boring — when the abstraction finally clicks and the system just works."

personality.label: IDE
personality.value: VS Code, but IntelliJ when Java gets serious

personality.label: Superpower
personality.value: Making a working MVP before the pitch deck is done

personality.label: Biggest flex
personality.value: Won a hackathon with 3 hours of sleep

projects.title: Inventory & Order Management
projects.description: A custom system for a local business — stock, orders, invoicing in one Flutter app, with a Firebase back end. Replaced a spreadsheet that nobody trusted anymore.

projects.title: Roux Institute Hackathon
projects.description: 2nd place. Built a tool for a healthcare partner in 36 hours — data ingestion, dashboard, and a demo that landed.

projects.title: Start Summit · Eco-Entrepreneur
projects.description: 1st place at the Bowdoin × Roux Institute hackathon — sustainability brief, a pitch that pitched itself, and a working MVP by Sunday morning.

projects.title: Telecom Test Simulator
projects.description: Java-based mock-API generator running against historical traffic. Turned a manual test cycle into a one-command run.

experience.description: Two semesters guiding undergrads through CS fundamentals — algorithms, data structures, the conversation you have with code at 2 a.m.
Built a Java testing simulator that mocked telecom-client APIs from historical data — 30% less testing time, 15 critical bugs caught pre-release. Rewrote review workflows for a 40% faster cycle.
One of seven freshers selected. Helped on web & graphic teams in the lead-up, then ran the backstage on event day — improvising under pressure.
Strategy + content for the national team. Seven cross-platform campaigns, six pieces of content a week, and a 20k follower bump.
